newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Only item in the window is a procedural 6-K: the annual shareholder meeting set for June 11, 2026 to vote on CEO compensation (base raise to ~NIS 1.34M, option/RSU grants) and auditor reappointment - not thesis-relevant. Prior catalysts (Q1 beat with $85.0M revenue vs $80.4M consensus, Roth Capital PT raise to $4.50 from $3.75, ~$86M YTD India orders) all predate June 6. Today's -3.4% move is low-liquidity small-cap drift, below the 5% threshold and not fundamental.

sources.label: TipRanks - Ceragon sets June 11, 2026 annual meeting (exec pay, auditor vote)
PR Newswire - Ceragon ~$86M India orders YTD reinforcing 2026 outlook (May 5, pre-window)
Ceragon press releases page (no new releases since June 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 38 on the scaling list: no new evidence on the wireless-backhaul/densification thesis since June 6, and today's -3.4% drift neither confirms nor breaks the setup. Yesterday's hold already reconciled the +31.5% YTD and $270M cap; the only fresh item (June 11 AGM on CEO pay) is procedural.
verdicts.bull: A $270M-cap microwave/mmWave backhaul pure-play with $85M Q1 revenue, ~$86M YTD India orders and a Roth PT raised to $4.50 - genuinely under-owned densification exposure with room if private-network and India momentum compounds.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 still printed a small adjusted net loss and the equity has already rerated +31.5% YTD on known catalysts; until new orders or guidance arrive, the alpha case is unchanged from yesterday.
verdicts.bear: Sub-$300M illiquid Israeli small-cap with thin margins; a North American order deferral already forced reliance on backlog timing, and any India order air-pocket or further margin pressure unwinds the YTD rerating quickly.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings and fresh order announcements (India operators, North American private networks) confirming the $355-385M FY26 revenue guide.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order timing slippage in India/North America plus margin pressure turning the FY26 guide stale, with low liquidity amplifying the downside.
